reasoning: A 1-week directional change in highly liquid bond/yield indices is historically very close to a random walk (approx. 50% base rate). Yields have recently spiked due to geopolitical tensions (e.g., Iran/oil shock), sending the AAA Corporate Yield to a local peak of 5.04% on March 27. While short-term momentum is upward, mean-reversion and the highly typical quarter-end/month-end portfolio rebalancing (where institutional funds buy bonds to meet target allocations after a bond market selloff) often place downward pressure on yields right around March 31/April 1. Coupled with the fact that the index must STRICTLY increase above its March 29 print to resolve YES, the probability is slightly below 50%.
